summary: Iter12 anti-overlap algorithm fully validated. The fix from iter11 (where Groupe 1 ⊂ 1/2 promo was incorrectly clusterized and subdivided the wider session to 25%) is correct. New rule: subdivision applies ONLY between sessions with EXACTLY the same lane range (same left+width groupKey). Wider blocks like 1/2 promo and promo entière keep their full lane width when co-existing with narrower contained sessions. 22 TEST sessions created across 5 days, all 6 spec cases verified via inline style.left/width inspection of session-block parents. 16/16 validations PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: PlanningGlobal.js ~628-706: refactored IIFE correctly uses groupKey = left.toFixed(2)|width.toFixed(2) for clustering. Only sessions with identical lane ranges form a subdivision group. Greedy interval-graph coloring per group → totalByIdx based on max col index of time-overlapping siblings in the same group. Correct and elegant.

Case 4 (user bug) confirmed fixed: 1/2 promo keeps 50% width and Groupe 1 keeps 12.5% width — they visually overlap on lanes 0..1 but this is now correctly treated as a DATA conflict (to be surfaced by Alertes) rather than masked by visual subdivision. Matches the iter12 design intent.

User case (1/2 promo + 7 sub-groups on lanes 8..14) confirmed: 1/2 promo = 0%/50%, each Gxx = own position/6.25% — totalByIdx=1 for all because no two sessions share exact same groupKey. This was the iter11 regression now fixed.
Case 5 (non-overlapping time, same lane range): both promo entière sessions placed in column 0 by greedy coloring, totalByIdx=1 each → full 100% width. Correct iter4-style stacking by time.
groupToLanes regex still caps at single digit (1..8). Library group 'Groupe 8a' must use letter form to be recognized; bare 'Groupe 10' would silently produce []. Not blocking but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: Created 7 new groups (Groupe 5a/5b/6a/6b/7a/7b/8a) tied to Promo 2026-2029, reused existing 1/2 promo + Groupe 1 + Groupe 2. Created 22 TEST_ITER12 sessions on 2026-12-07..11. ALL cleaned up at end of run: 22/22 sessions DELETEd + 7/7 created groups DELETEd. Database left in original state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter12 layout/anti-overlap verified by inspecting inline style of session-block PARENT (.absolute wrapper). Measured left%/width% values matched expectations exactly. Results: USER case → 1/2 promo (0%/50%) + 7×(own_lane/6.25%); C1 → 3×(0,33.33,66.67)/33.33% ; C2 → 5×(0,2.5,5,7.5,10)/2.5% ; C3 → G1(0%/12.5%) + G2(12.5%/12.5%) ; C4 → 1/2 promo(0%/50%) + G1(0%/12.5%) (visually overlap = real data conflict, not masked); C5 → both promo entière at 0%/100% (no time overlap). Reproduce by POST /api/sessions with promotion_id + group_ids, navigate /planning-global, set date-picker + click filter-promo-{pid}, then page.evaluate parentElement.style of each [data-testid=session-block-{id}].
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bug found. Iter12 fix for iter11 regression is correct. The algorithmic change (group by exact lane-range key vs lane-overlap BFS cluster) properly preserves wider session widths while still subdividing identical-lane-range siblings. Case 4 visual overlap is intentional behavior signaling a data-level conflict to be handled by Alertes, not by the layout engine.
